title: FedEx's (FDX) Pilots Vote Against Provisional Agreement
text: Following the adverse voting outcome, negotiations between FedEx's (FDX) pilots' union and the company, under the supervision of the National Mediation Board, are likely.

title: Amazon, FedEx, US Postal Service prep as potential UPS strike looms
text: Shipping companies like Amazon, FedEx and the U.S. Postal Service are monitoring the evolving negotiations between UPS and the Teamsters union as a potential strike looms.

title: FedEx pilots reject 30% pay hike proposal, but a strike isn't imminent

title: FedEx Stock Is Rising. New Management and UPS Woes Are the Reasons.
text: Investors seem happy with FedEx's new chief financial officer. Also, the logistics giant—which has a mostly nonunion workforce—would gain from a potential strike at rival United Parcel Service.

title: FedEx names its new CFO
text: FedEx Corp. FDX, -1.01% said Monday it has named John W. Dietrich, the former chief executive officer of cargo airline Atlas Air Worldwide, its next chief financial officer, effective Aug. 1.

title: FedEx names John W. Dietrich as Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer
text: MEMPHIS, Tenn.--(BUSINESS WIRE)--FedEx Corp. (NYSE: FDX) today announced the appointment of John W. Dietrich, formerly the Chief Executive Officer of Atlas Air Worldwide, to Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer, effective Aug. 1, 2023. The company also announced strategic shifts within the Finance organization to further bolster its ongoing transformation. As previously announced, Executive Vice President and Chief Financial Officer Michael C. Lenz will transition out of his rol.

title: FedEx advises UPS shippers to get on board now
text: FedEx Corp. advised shippers on Thursday that they should begin shipping now with the company if they are concerned about service disruptions arising from a possible Teamsters union strike Aug. 1 of UPS Inc. In a communique Thursday, FedEx (NYSE: FDX) said that the company's priority is “protecting capacity and service for existing customers.” Customers considering shifting volumes to FedEx, or currently in discussions with the company to open a new account, are “encouraged to begin shipping with FedEx now,” the communique said.
